title: rumex infestus
intro: Rumex infestus, also known as the field sorrel or sheep's sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high levels of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant has been used to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, and its antioxidant properties have been shown to have anti-cancer and anti-aging effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis, gastritis, and skin conditions.
